I'm a frequent customer, but unfortunately, my last experience was very bad and disappointing.
I bought a children's train toy for 50,000 dinars. When I tested it at home, it sometimes worked and other times stopped. The malfunctions were frequent, both with the remote control and the train itself.
I went back to them to exchange the toy, and when they turned it on and it worked normally, they refused to replace it, even though I clearly explained that the toy sometimes works and sometimes malfunctions.
The staff's behavior was vulgar and inappropriate, as if we were in a "market place": "Leave it for an hour, and if something happens to it, we'll replace it!" It was as if the customer, once they pay for the toy, becomes indebted and at their mercy!
I left the toy with them and left, and the seller didn't even attempt to resolve the issue in a decent manner.
They have no conscience, and 50,000 isn't worth the anguish of a little child.
I will never buy from them again, and I don't recommend anyone to buy from them.

reviewsSample.text:
With hurried steps and a racing heart, I began my first day at university, drawn in by the motto, “We challenge the present to shape the future.”

The threshold of the door felt like the future that would define my new life.

I hugged my books, smiling as if it were my long-awaited day. I began to imagine and wonder, “What does a university professor look like?”

I pictured him in my mind: thick hair, heavy glasses, and clothes dusted with chalk.

My breath caught in my throat as I approached my college. Finally, it was the College of Information Technology! I started to wander through the halls, feeling lost and confused. I saw new faces, many smiles… that young man was looking at his schedule full of lectures, and that girl was asking about her classroom.

Back then, we were called “the Smurfs” because we were new. I tried to pretend I was a seasoned student in this college so I wouldn't fall for the pranks of the older students.

And finally, I found my classroom!

There was the teacher, not quite what I'd imagined, but he wore thick glasses… He looked at us and said, “Anyone who can't write a program will fail the course, even if they get a 100!”

I don't know! Perhaps it was his way of welcoming the “Smurfs”… I mean, the new students!

It all began at that door, behind which many secrets were hidden. I didn't realize then that I was in a new world, so different from school.

University!

That was the beginning of a new life, with many faces, huge, towering buildings unlike my small school. The seats were full of students, some engrossed in their studies, others lost in thoughtful glances at the girl sitting alone.

As for me, I was absorbed in exploring the college building and its scattered lecture halls. It was a wonderful feeling; I had entered my favorite major.

It's university life, and everything within it has a different meaning. Even the smallest details are beautiful. Many enter this life: some searching for fleeting love, others seeking solace from the intellectual exhaustion that has plagued them for years, and still others building their own paths to success, becoming future leaders.

It's a life whose chapters unfold over the years.

University wasn't like my childhood dreams, not like all the descriptions I'd heard. It's changed drastically from what you know. It's not to blame; we are.

Today's university student doesn't contribute to maintaining their university in the slightest! Even the cleanliness of the university has become a source of annoyance to many. You'll still find them throwing cigarette butts on the ground, leaving their food and drink remnants in the hallways, classrooms, and under seats, and speaking with a foul mouth and an accent only the ignorant and backward would use.
